name: Vallvidrera and Tibidabo Loop
name_original: La Floresta y les Planes  – vuelta por el Parque Natural de Collserola
description: This moderate 10.9-mile road cycling route through Collserola Natural Park offers panoramic views of Barcelona and challenging climbs.
region_intro: Road cycling in Serra De Collserola offers a diverse landscape for no traffic road cycling routes, characterized by expansive Mediterranean forests, rolling hills, and significant ascents. The natural park, often referred to as "the lungs of Barcelona," features a network of well-paved roads that wind through varied terrain. Cyclists can expect routes with notable elevation gains, providing both challenging climbs and scenic descents. High points within the park offer panoramic views over Barcelona and the surrounding region. Best no traffic road cycling routes in Serra De Collserola The most popular no traffic road cycling route is La Roca Road – Cànoves loop from Montcada i Reixac - Manresa, a 47.4 miles (76.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route features over 2,100 feet (640 m) of elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Roca – Besòs River Park loop from Casa de l'Aigua, a moderate 32.5 miles (52.2 km) path. This route offers a mix of riverside sections and climbs, providing diverse scenery. Local road cyclists also love the La Conreria Pass – Conreria Pass loop from Cerdanyola Universitat, a 29.3 miles (47.2 km) trail leading through the Collserola hills, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling in Serra De Collserola is defined by Mediterranean forests, significant hills, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult routes with substantial climbing. The routes in Serra De Collserola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 1,100 reviews. More than 70,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Serra De Collserola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no traffic road cycling routes are available in Serra de Collserola?
A: Serra de Collserola offers a wide selection of car-free road cycling routes. You'll find over 50 routes specifically designed for road bikes with minimal traffic, ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: Are there options for beginner-friendly no traffic road cycling routes?
A: Yes, beginners can enjoy several no traffic road cycling routes in Serra de Collserola. There are 18 easy routes that offer a gentler introduction to the park's beautiful scenery without significant climbs. These routes often feature well-paved surfaces, making them ideal for a relaxed ride.

Q: Are there challenging no traffic road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Serra de Collserola provides 8 difficult no traffic road cycling routes. These routes often include significant elevation gains, such as the climb to Tibidabo, which can reach over 3,000 feet with sections exceeding a 20% gradient. An example is the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Llorenç Savall – Gallecs Rural Area loop from Cerdanyola del Vallès&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 90 km with more than 1,100 meters of ascent.

Q: What is the best time of year for no traffic road cycling in Serra de Collserola?
A: The Mediterranean climate of Serra de Collserola makes it suitable for road cycling almost year-round. Spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are generally mild, though some higher sections might be cooler.

Q: Are there circular no traffic road cycling routes in Serra de Collserola?
A: Yes, many of the no traffic road cycling routes in Serra de Collserola are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34632304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Roca Road – Cànoves loop from Montcada i Reixac - Manresa&lt;/a&gt; offers a 76 km circular ride with moderate difficulty.

Q: What can I see along the no traffic road cycling routes?
A: The routes in Serra de Collserola offer stunning panoramic views of Barcelona, the Llobregat delta, and the surrounding mountains. You can also discover natural features like lush Mediterranean forests and diverse ecosystems. Notable landmarks and viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1438194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cypresses Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2687128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pere Màrtir Summit and Observation Towers&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the routes?
A: Yes, Serra de Collserola is home to several historical and cultural sites. You might encounter charming old churches or the Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1059270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Creu d'Olorda Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, which often serves as a pleasant stop with a restaurant and picnic area. The park also features the iconic Collserola communications tower and the Temple Expiatori del Sagrat Cor near Tibidabo.

Q: How can I access the no traffic road cycling routes using public transport?
A: Serra de Collserola is well-connected to Barcelona by public transport. Various train (FGC, Rodalies) and bus lines reach different access points of the park, making it convenient to start your no traffic road cycling adventure without a car. For instance, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besòs Riverside Park – Besòs River Park loop from Montcada i Reixac&lt;/a&gt; are easily accessible from Montcada i Reixac.

Q: Is parking available near the no traffic road cycling routes in Serra de Collserola?
A: Yes, there are various parking areas available at different access points around Serra de Collserola, especially in the towns bordering the park. These allow you to park your car and begin your no traffic road cycling route. It's advisable to check specific starting points for parking availability.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the no traffic road cycling routes?
A: Yes, you'll find several spots for refreshments within and around Serra de Collserola. The Santa Creu d'Olorda Hermitage, for example, has a restaurant. Additionally, many routes pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find cafes and shops to refuel during your ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about no traffic road cycling in Serra de Collserola?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the quiet, well-paved roads that feel far removed from urban life, the challenging yet rewarding climbs, and the breathtaking panoramic views over Barcelona and the surrounding natural park. The diverse terrain and lush Mediterranean forests also contribute to a highly enjoyable cycling experience.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints worth visiting on these routes?
A: Serra de Collserola is famous for its numerous 'miradors' or viewpoints. High points like Tibidabo Mountain offer spectacular vistas. Other notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1438194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cypresses Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, Mirador de Sarrià, Mirador de l'Arrabassada, and Mirador dels Xiprers, all providing excellent photo opportunities and places to rest.
tips: If you were a cyclist from outside Barcelona, the viewpoint of Vallvidirera is a nice balcony where you can observe panoramic views of part of the city of Barcelona and the mouth of the Llobregat river.
Alternative route to avoid traffic, watch out for a hole.
And after this, I recommend to go have a coffee and a brownie at Les Escales 🙂
A nice little cafe in La Floresta. Here you can find coffee, sandwhiches, empanadas, salads, and soups. It has options for vegetarians and vegans.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily designed for road cycling, featuring mostly asphalt and paved sections. However, be aware that some segments within the Collserola Natural Park may be unpaved and could be challenging for a race bike. It requires good fitness due to twisting climbs and descents, with some sections reaching 10% or even over 20% on approaches to areas like Tibidabo.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' and requires good cycling fitness. While it offers a rewarding experience, some climbs can be steep. If you're a beginner, consider building up your fitness on flatter routes before tackling this one.

faqs.question: What kind of views and landmarks can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy exceptional scenery and panoramic views of Barcelona and the coastline. A notable viewpoint is the View of Barcelona from Carretera de Vallvidrera. The route also passes through the vast Mediterranean vegetation of the Collserola Natural Park, and you might spot landmarks like the Torre de Collserola in the distance.

faqs.question: Are there any good spots for a break or refreshment on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can find local spots for a break. The Bistró Les Escales in La Floresta is known for homemade pastries and coffee, making it an excellent refreshment stop.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Collserola Natural Park is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be hot, so an early morning ride is advisable, while winters are mild but can be damp.

faqs.question: How do I get to the start of the route, and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is easily accessible from Barcelona, providing an urban escape into nature. You can access La Floresta by public transport, or if driving, look for available parking in the La Floresta or Les Planes areas. Specific parking details may vary, so check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are there any specific rules or permits needed for cycling in Collserola Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Collserola Natural Park is a protected area. While no specific cycling permits are typically required for general use, a speed limit of 20 km/h is enforced on cycling paths, and cycling is prohibited on footpaths. Always practice responsible cycling to protect the environment.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Collserola Natural Park generally allows dogs, this specific route is designed for race biking on roads and some potentially unpaved sections. Bringing a dog along on a race bike can be challenging and is not typically recommended for this type of activity. If you wish to explore the park with a dog, consider dedicated hiking or gravel biking trails where it's safer and more suitable for your pet.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths in Collserola?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the extensive cycling network within the Collserola Natural Park. It intersects with sections of various paths, including Carenejant Collserola, De l'Arrabassada al Tibidabo, el Centre d'Informació del Parc, Vil·la Joana i Vallvidrera, and L'Obaga de Collserola, among others. This allows for potential extensions or variations to your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, bring your race bike in good condition, a helmet, water, and snacks. Consider a repair kit and pump. Wear appropriate cycling attire, and if you plan to stop at a cafe like Bistró Les Escales, bring some cash or a card.

faqs.question: Are there any unpaved sections on this route that might be difficult for a race bike?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily a road cycling route, some segments within the Collserola Natural Park may be unpaved. These sections can be challenging for a race bike, so be prepared for varied surfaces and potentially slower going in those areas. It's a good idea to check your tires and bike setup accordingly.
